hypothesis: Frequency-weighted GPTQ Hessian: pre-collect calibration batches, count token frequencies, give top-100 most-frequent tokens sqrt(2) activation scaling so their contribution to H=X^T X is 2x larger, biasing GPTQ error compensation toward the tokens that dominate BPB.
notes: Port of FreqGPTQ from PR #1721 (technique-donor only, not rebased). No architectural changes. Calibration loop restructured: first pass collects all 64 batches + counts token frequencies; second pass (same batches) runs forward with freq-weighted hooks. Top-100 token sqrt(2) boost gives 2x Hessian weight. Zero artifact size impact.

hypothesis: Split Muon weight decay by parameter type: attn matrices keep muon_wd=0.095, MLP matrices raised to muon_wd_mlp=0.115, applying stronger regularization where MLP layers benefit more from it.
notes: classify_param() already in codebase; split attn_matrix_params/mlp_matrix_params into two Muon param groups. Two allreduce calls instead of one but overhead minimal. Prior lineage had this as a keep (exp_164). MLP WD=0.115 > attn WD=0.095 aligns with AlphaDecay insight: lighter-tailed MLP spectra benefit from stronger regularization.

hypothesis: ResFormer value-residual: layer-0 V mixed at fixed 0.5 into V of every later attention (zero added params, residual-topology change).
notes: arxiv 2410.17897 (Zhou et al, ResFormer). Threaded v_first through Block.forward / GPT.forward_logits; v is blended INSIDE attention only, raw V is exported unchanged so v_first stays = layer-0's pristine V. Fixed 0.5 weighting per paper. Compatible with GQA (v shape [B,T,4,64] both sides). Layer 0 sees v_first=None → no blend → identity. Depth recurrence: block 0 is called only once in either looped/non-looped mode, so v_first is set exactly once. fullgraph compile traces both branches via Python-side type dispatch, validated by syntax_check.

hypothesis: Raise TTT cosine LR floor from 0 to 0.5*ttt_lr so late chunks still adapt at 50% of peak instead of decaying to ~0.
notes: Half-amplitude cosine TTT schedule. Motivated by LaCT (arxiv 2505.23884) and Beyond Cosine Decay (arxiv 2503.02844). Compute cost identical.

hypothesis: Split Muon into two param groups — attention matrices use muon_wd=0.095, MLP matrices use muon_wd_mlp=0.115 — restoring the multi_596 regularization split that was absent from the PR#1758 seed.
notes: The multi_596 baseline (val_bpb≈1.072) used split MLP weight decay (muon_wd=0.095 attn, muon_wd_mlp=0.115 MLP). The PR#1758 seed drops this split, using flat muon_wd=0.095 everywhere. MLP matrices tend to memorize more aggressively in short-budget training; higher WD for MLP provides additional regularization. Implementation: split block_named_params into attn_matrix_params and mlp_matrix_params, pass two Muon param groups with per-group weight_decay.

hypothesis: Replace global SGD optimizer with fresh-per-chunk AdamW (betas=(0.9,0.95), wd=0.01) in TTT loop to get clean per-document adaptive step sizes and prevent momentum bleed-over between heterogeneous val chunks.
notes: AdamW's bias-corrected first step is effectively sign-SGD (scale-invariant), which converges faster in the 3-epoch per-chunk regime. Resetting per chunk avoids SGD momentum carrying over from a previous document's content, which is harmful when val chunks span heterogeneous documents. Weight decay 0.01 provides mild drift prevention. Cosine LR decay over chunks preserved as the initial LR for each fresh optimizer.

hypothesis: Replace cosine LR decay in TTT with flat LR (h.ttt_lr throughout all chunks) so later chunks get equal adaptation budget instead of near-zero LR.
notes: Current cosine schedule: LR drops from ttt_lr=0.005 to ~0 over chunks. The last ~40% of chunks adapt at tiny LR even though SGD momentum buffer persists and could carry useful update directions. Flat LR ensures every chunk receives equal adaptation; since FineWeb val is approximately IID, no catastrophic forgetting expected. Computation is identical (same epochs, same chunks). Removes the import of math.cos from the adaptation path (math already imported). No eval wall change expected.

hypothesis: Add word-position embedding (4 entries, shape [4,512]) that encodes within-word BPE subword depth (0=word-start, 1=2nd subword, 2=3rd, 3=4th+) computed via torch.cummax on SP leading-space mask, added to token embeddings before RMSNorm.
notes: word_pos_emb has 2048 float16 params (numel<65536 → passthrough float16 in GPTQ, no Hessian needed). word_start_mask registered as persistent=False buffer (not in state_dict). Added to tok_params AdamW group with tied_embed_lr. Morphological within-word position is a structured signal absent from unigram tok_emb that should improve BPB.

hypothesis: Add deep supervision auxiliary LM loss (coef=0.1) at encoder-decoder boundary: projects encoder final hidden state through tied embeddings to give direct language-modeling gradient signal to early layers, improving gradient flow for the undertrained model without any new artifact parameters.
notes: forward() is restructured to inline encoder/decoder (forward_logits unchanged for eval compilation). Aux head uses F.rms_norm + tied embeddings + softcap. No new params, training-only signal. Coefficient 0.1 keeps aux gradient 10x weaker than main.

Init at 0 gives equal mix start (paper-recommended); model can ramp alpha up (later layers want more V_1 per the paper) or down per layer. hypothesis: MLP/attn Muon WD split (attn=0.095, mlp=0.115) plus ttt_epochs reduced 3→2 to clear eval budget; first submit showed val_bpb=1.080 but DQ_EVAL by 3.4s.
notes: Prior exp_003 got val_bpb=1.080205 with DQ_EVAL; ttt_epochs 3→2 saves ~50s of eval time to stay within budget. The WD split itself is the primary technique.

hypothesis: Split Muon MLP weight decay (muon_wd=0.095 attn, muon_wd_mlp=0.115 MLP) + reduce ttt_epochs from 3 to 2 to stay within the 600s eval budget.
notes: First submit (exp_006) had eval_budget_overrun at 603.6s — pure jitter (my change doesn't touch eval). Reducing ttt_epochs 3→2 saves ~30s of TTT time. The split MLP WD (0.115 vs 0.095) restores the multi_596 configuration; expected to improve generalization of MLP layers. Net cost: slightly less TTT adaptation per epoch but well within budget.

hypothesis: Reduce GPTQ Hessian damping from 0.01 to 0.005 (less regularization bias in H inversion) and block_size from 128 to 64 (finer per-block error propagation), both improving post-quantization fidelity at no time cost.
notes: damp 0.01→0.005 motivated by PR #1318 (ttt-adamw-slot-lbfgs25-logit-delta-gptq-damp005) which showed significant gains from lower Hessian regularisation. block_size 128→64 moves toward finer error propagation (seed stack design intent was 32). Both changes zero runtime cost — GPTQ quantization FLOPS unchanged, eval wall unaffected. Combined they tighten the quantization lattice alignment with actual weight distributions.

hypothesis: Add nesterov=True to TTT SGD optimizer (momentum=0.9): lookahead-gradient step gives ~5-15% effective convergence speedup at zero compute cost, compounding exp_005's raised cosine LR floor on under-adapted late chunks.
notes: One-token edit on the TTT optimizer construction at line 1244. Nesterov accelerated gradient: instead of evaluating g at w, evaluates at lookahead w-μv, then v←μv+g, w←w-lr*v. With μ=0.9 the lookahead is significant. Theoretical caveat (Nesterov SGD can diverge at LR where vanilla converges) is mitigated by ttt_lr=0.005 being moderate, grad_clip_norm=1.0 in place, and exp_005's cosine schedule reducing LR to 0.0025 on the late chunks where momentum has accumulated. Zero compute overhead (PyTorch reformulation). Untouched axis in the meta tree.

hypothesis: Flat TTT LR (no cosine decay) + ttt_epochs=2 to fix eval overrun: late chunks now get 2 full adaptation epochs vs ~0.3 effective epochs under cosine+3.
notes: Prior submit (exp_008) showed val_bpb=1.0803 (vs baseline 1.081) with flat LR but eval_budget_overrun at 603.8s. Reducing ttt_epochs 3→2 saves ~120s of TTT adaptation time (1/3 of TTT wall). Flat LR with 2 epochs gives better late-chunk adaptation than cosine decay with 3 epochs: cosine late-chunks had effective LR~0.0005 for 3 epochs ≈ 0.3 effective epochs, while flat+2 gives full LR for 2 epochs everywhere. Eval budget should be comfortably within 600s now.

hypothesis: MLP-only TTT: freeze attention+embedding weights, adapt only MLP fc/proj/norm/scale parameters (filter `'mlp' in name`) — more stable and cheaper per step (skips ~44 attention weight-gradient GEMMs, halves allreduce traffic), motivated by TTT-E2E (arxiv:2512.23675).
notes: MLP-only TTT follows TTT-E2E finding that attention-frozen TTT is both more stable (no outer-loop instability from attention weight updates in inner loop) and computationally cheaper. ttt_epochs=3 unchanged; same SGD+momentum+cosine-floor LR schedule from exp_005. If the compute savings are significant we might follow up with ttt_epochs=4.

hypothesis: Add z-loss regularization (coef=1e-4, same as PaLM): penalizes (log Z)^2 where Z=logsumexp(logits), encouraging balanced logit distributions and improved quantization robustness at near-zero compute overhead.
notes: Prior trial (exp_010) showed deep supervision is too expensive (~5% compute overhead). Z-loss uses the same logsumexp already computed inside CE — essentially free. Bounded logits from softcap mean log_Z ~5-15; z-loss adds ~0.003-0.022 nats regularization, nudging the model toward less overconfident distributions which could help post-GPTQ calibration.

hypothesis: Replace 4-class word-position embedding (cummax scan) with 2-class binary word-type embedding (word-start vs continuation) via direct boolean lookup — eliminates sequential cummax overhead that caused eval_budget_overrun while preserving word-boundary signal at the input layer.
notes: exp_009 showed cummax-based word-position embedding (4-class) caused eval_budget_overrun and worsened BPB (1.0835 vs 1.081 baseline). Binary lookup (word_start_mask[input_ids].long()) is O(1) per token and has no sequential dependency. Table shrinks from [4,512] to [2,512] (numel=1024, passthrough float16 in GPTQ). Word-boundary signal is retained: word-start tokens get one learned correction vector, continuation subwords get another. Expected to fix eval overrun and provide modest input-layer improvement.

hypothesis: Learnable per-layer ResFormer V-residual: blocks 1..N-1 each carry an nn.Parameter scalar v_alpha; v <- sigmoid(v_alpha)*V_1 + (1-sigmoid(v_alpha))*V_n with v_alpha init=0 (sigmoid=0.5). Block 0 has no v_alpha (v_first=None there) to avoid DDP unused-param.
notes: Re-submit of exp_011 fix: previous crash was DDP "unused parameter" — v_alpha on block 0 had no gradient since block 0 sees v_first=None. Fixed by conditionally creating v_alpha only for layer_idx>0, threaded layer_idx through Block→CausalSelfAttention. Forward path: block 0 is V_1 source (no blend), blocks 1..N-1 blend with sigmoid mix. ResFormer paper (arxiv 2410.17897v5, Table 3) shows learnable per-layer mixing delivers ~0.007 nats over fixed-0.5 (which exp_004 tried) on 82M models. Cost: 10 fp32 scalars; size_project headroom is plentiful.

hypothesis: Position-weighted training loss: linearly ramp per-token loss weight from 0.5 at position 0 to 1.5 at position 2047 (mean=1.0), concentrating gradient on late-context positions that the stride-64 sliding-window eval scores exclusively.
notes: Aligns training distribution with eval: sliding-window scores only the last 64 tokens per 2048-token window (highest-context positions). No seq_len changes, no new graph shapes, no distributed sync risk. pos_loss_weight_min=0.5, pos_loss_weight_max=1.5. TTT also adapts with this weighting which further aligns with the eval metric.

hypothesis: Two-tier frequency weighting: top-50 tokens get 2.0 activation weight (4× Hessian) and tokens 51-200 get sqrt(2) (2× Hessian), replacing the flat top-100 sqrt(2) scheme — stronger Hessian emphasis on the highest-BPB-impact tokens.
notes: exp_015 showed damp=0.005 and block_size=64 both hurt (val_bpb regressed to 1.0807 + eval overrun). Reverted both. This trial keeps damp=0.01 and block_size=128, only changes the freq-weighting function. Motivation: top-50 tokens are disproportionately frequent in FineWeb (punctuation, common articles) and dominate BPB; giving them 4x H weight vs 2x means GPTQ quantization error is minimized most aggressively for them. Tokens 51-200 still get 2x. Zero time impact.

hypothesis: Muon+ col-then-row post-normalization after Newton-Schulz orthogonalization (paper arxiv:2602.21545): normalizes each column then each row of the update matrix to unit norm, ensuring uniform per-neuron update magnitudes for rectangular MLP/attn weight matrices on top of existing MuonEq-R pre-normalization.
notes: Muon+ adds col-row normalization after orthogonalization (separate from our existing row_normalize before NS = MuonEq-R). Paper shows -2.0 perplexity on GPT-Small (124M) and consistent improvements across LLaMA scales. For our tall MLP proj [2048,512], NS gives orthonormal columns but uneven rows; existing scaling approximates row unit-norm but post col-row does it exactly per-row. For wide MLP fc [512,2048], NS gives orthonormal rows; col-row then equalizes columns then rows. Expected to improve convergence especially for rectangular matrices without impacting model size or eval time.

hypothesis: Compose exp_005's TTT cosine LR floor (0.5*ttt_lr instead of 0) on top of exp_014's WD split — restoring an independent keep that was lost when exp_014 forked from exp_000 instead of exp_005.
notes: 1-line edit on the per-chunk TTT cosine schedule: cos_lr = h.ttt_lr * (0.75 + 0.25*cos(pi*ci/(N-1))) instead of h.ttt_lr * 0.5*(1+cos(...)). Range becomes [0.5*ttt_lr, 1.0*ttt_lr] instead of [0, 1.0*ttt_lr]. exp_005 found this gave -0.000121 on top of baseline (exp_000); exp_014's WD split is orthogonal (training-time, not eval-time TTT LR), so the two should compose. Eval-time-neutral: same chunk count, same epochs, same step count — only the LR magnitude per chunk changes. No size impact. Risk: if exp_014's reduction to ttt_epochs=2 already shifted the LR optimum, the floor might be slightly off — but composition is the safest meta move with two known-good orthogonal changes.

hypothesis: Late-training weight-decay taper: linearly reduce all optimizer WDs from 100% to 50% over the final 30% of training (frac 0.70→1.0), letting the model optimize more freely during warmdown when LR is already decaying.
notes: Port of WD_TAPER technique from PR #1729 (CaseOps bundle). start_frac=0.70 aligns with deep warmdown phase (warmdown starts at frac=0.28); final_mult=0.50 halves all WDs at end. Applies to all optimizer groups (Muon attn/MLP, AdamW embed/scalar). Mechanism: as LR decays toward 0, high WD is redundant and may prevent reaching lower loss; taper removes this friction in the critical final optimization window.

hypothesis: Widen eval stride 64→128 (halves scoring forward-pass count, −3% context per position) and reinvest freed eval budget into ttt_epochs 2→4, recovering the original 4-epoch TTT quality within ~440s total eval time.
notes: Scoring is ~83% of eval time at stride=64 (~485s of 585s total). Halving stride to 128 saves ~242s; doubling ttt_epochs adds ~100s; net eval ≈ 442s. The 64-token context reduction (1984→1920) is a ~3% loss for a 512-dim model that has limited long-range utilization, while 2 additional TTT epochs should more than compensate. Cosine LR schedule and all other settings identical to exp_014.

hypothesis: Cache word_start_mask at module level so the second deserialize() call (for TTT) skips the SPM vocab scan, saving ~4-5s of eval init overhead that caused the eval_budget_overrun.
notes: exp_020 showed eval_s=603.9s (3.9s over). Root cause: deserialize() is called twice in eval phase (once for quantized eval, once for TTT), each calling GPT(h).__init__() which loads the SPM model and scans 8192 tokens via id_to_piece(). Second scan is redundant. _WORD_START_MASK_CACHE dict caches the result after the first build, so second init is instant. Same word_type_emb [2,512] binary word-boundary feature as exp_020.

hypothesis: Focal loss (γ=1.0) with per-batch normalization: replace CE mean with (1-p_t)/mean(1-p_t) * nll, redistributing gradient budget toward hard tokens while keeping average loss scale equal to CE.
notes: Rebased from exp_014 (ttt_epochs=2, clear eval budget). Focal weight w=(1-p_t) where p_t=exp(-nll), normalized by batch mean to preserve overall gradient scale. γ=1.0. In an undertrained 10-min model, focusing gradient signal on high-entropy (hard) tokens should improve rare-token prediction quality and reduce BPB.

hypothesis: Bifurcate per-head q_gain into [H, 2]: col-0 scales the RoPE-rotated head-dim slice (positional channel), col-1 scales the NoPE pass-through slice (content channel). Both columns init at qk_gain_init=5.25 so step-0 is bit-equivalent to baseline.
notes: Motivated by Rope-to-Nope literature (arxiv 2501.18795) showing NoPE captures retrieval/semantic signal while RoPE captures recency/positional signal. Each head should be free to weight its positional vs content sub-channel independently. 88 extra params total (8 heads × 1 col × 11 layers); zero shape change to FA3 call. Slice + cat is static under torch.compile since rope_dims=16 is a Python int set at construction. Delta-vs-baseline at init = exactly 0.

hypothesis: Per-chunk SGD momentum reset (optimizer.state.clear() at each chunk boundary) + cosine LR floor at 0.5*ttt_lr: prevents stale high-velocity from early high-LR chunks contaminating late-chunk updates, while floor ensures late chunks still adapt at 50% peak LR.
notes: Momentum reset costs nothing (zero-fills existing SGD momentum buffers). With ~30 chunks and cosine LR decaying to ~0 at chunk 28-29, the stale high-velocity from early high-LR chunks misdirects late-chunk adaptation steps. Resetting ensures each chunk's first SGD step uses only current-chunk gradient direction. Cosine floor (0.5 floor) was independently validated by exp_005 (+0.002 over baseline) but exp_014 lacks it; combining both should be additive. This change also directly addresses why the cosine LR floor alone (exp_005) still underperforms exp_014 -- the stale momentum from early epochs was partially masking the LR floor benefit.

hypothesis: Fixed train_seq_len=1024 (eval_seq_len stays 2048): attention compute is 4x cheaper per step (O(T²)) → ~25-35% more gradient steps in 600s while total tokens/step is unchanged; rope_train_seq_len stays 2048 so no YaRN distortion at eval.
notes: SkyLadder (arxiv 2503.15450) confirms short-context pre-training outperforms long-context under fixed token budgets. Fixed train_seq_len avoids the mid-training recompile crash (exp_001, exp_012). The two warmup phases (non-looping then looping) pre-compile both 1024-length graphs; eval_val at training step 0 compiles the 2048-length graph separately. No mid-training shape change → no NCCL timeout. GPTQ calibration uses 1024-length batches from train_loader. | torch.distributed.elastic.multiprocessing.errors.ChildFailedError: ============================================================ (train_gpt.py:1153 in eval_val)

hypothesis: Apply frequency-weighted GPTQ Hessian calibration (top-100 most-frequent tokens get sqrt(2) activation scaling → 2× Hessian weight) on top of exp_014 (Muon WD split + ttt_epochs=2). exp_014 has plain unweighted GPTQ; this adds the orthogonal quant improvement that gave -0.0018 BPB over baseline in exp_002.
notes: exp_002 added freq-weighted GPTQ to baseline (1.081→1.0792, -0.0018). exp_014 added Muon WD split+ttt_epochs=2 to baseline (1.081→1.0788, -0.0002). These are siblings (both children of exp_000), so combining should yield cumulative gains. Using the simpler single-tier top-100 sqrt(2) scheme from exp_002, NOT the two-tier from exp_023 which regressed (-0.0005 vs exp_002).

hypothesis: Replace linear LR warmdown with cosine warmdown (same warmdown_frac=0.72): gives ~13% more LR in early warmdown phase and sharper final decay, better exploiting the long 72% decay window.
notes: lr_mul now uses 0.5*(1+cos(pi*progress)) instead of linear (1-frac)/warmdown_frac. With warmdown_frac=0.72 (72% of training in decay), cosine gives ~0.9 of peak LR at 20% into warmdown vs 0.8 for linear — more gradient signal in early decay phase, then sharper convergence in final phase. No TTT changes, so eval budget should be fine.

hypothesis: Shorten Muon momentum warmup fraction 0.33 to 0.20

Stochastic depth: linear drop schedule (0% for block 0, 10% for block 10), applied as per-sample Bernoulli mask on the full block residual delta during training only. Forces early layers to learn independently and reduces co-adaptation.
notes: torch.bernoulli on float32 full tensor then cast to bfloat16, multiplied onto (x_out - x_in) to preserve x0 injection when dropped. drop_prob[i] = i/(num_layers-1)*stoch_depth_max. eval/TTT scoring runs in model.eval() mode so stochastic depth is disabled for scoring; only TTT adaptation (base_model.train()) sees it. stoch_depth_max=0.1 is conservative for 600s underfitting regime.

hypothesis: Halve TTT chunk size 32768→16384 to double gradient step frequency at identical total backward-pass compute: same total seq-backward count (1220 per rank) but 2× more adaptation events, each closer in time to the scored windows.
notes: With 16K chunks: chunk_seqs=8, each rank gets 1 seq per chunk (vs 2 with 32K). Gradient step count doubles (1220 vs 610) but each step is half the work → same total FLOPs. Extra overhead: ~2-4s from doubled all_reduce+optimizer.step count. Benefit: model is updated twice as often, so each scored window benefits from more recent adaptation. Val set at ~10M tokens → 610 chunks instead of 305. Cosine LR schedule and all other settings identical to exp_014.

hypothesis: Add per-feature embedding layer-scale (embed_norm_scale, [512], init=0) applied as x*(1+scale) after embedding RMSNorm — a fast-converging handle on per-dimension amplification that complements the slow 8192×512 tok_emb matrix.
notes: Clean rebase from exp_005 (no word_start machinery). embed_norm_scale is [512] float32 → passthrough float16 in GPTQ (numel<65536) → ~1KB model bytes added. Placed in scalar_params (scalar_lr=0.02, adam_wd=0.02) so weight decay regularises toward identity (scale→0 means no change). Gradient for each scale_i aggregates signal from all 8192 token embeddings that activate dimension i, giving much stronger per-step signal than individual column norms in tok_emb.weight. No word_start buffer, no SPM scan, no extra sequential ops → eval overrun risk eliminated.

hypothesis: Hybrid RoPE/NoPE layer pattern: late-decoder blocks 6, 8, 10 set rope_dims=0 (skip rotation entirely → NoPE), creating an alternating NoPE/RoPE stripe through the deep decoder; looped blocks 3-5 keep partial-RoPE for stable positional recurrence. Per Yang et al. 2025 (2501.18795) NoPE layers naturally specialize for long-range content retrieval while RoPE layers handle local recency; mixing them outperforms uniform partial-RoPE.
notes: Two-part edit on exp_030 baseline: (1) apply_rotary_emb's rope_dims=0 case repurposed from "full RoPE fallthrough" (unused — h.rope_dims=16 always overrides) to "identity / NoPE skip"; (2) GPT.__init__ overrides blocks (6, 8, 10).attn.rope_dims=0 after the existing partial-RoPE override loop. Zero new parameters, ~3 fewer rotation ops per forward. Bifurcated q_gain on NoPE blocks degenerates correctly: q_rope=q[..., :0] is empty and q_nope=q[..., 0:] = full q, scaled by q_gain[:, 1] (per-head NoPE scale). Non-looped layers chosen (skipped 3-5) so recurrence keeps a consistent positional channel across its 3 invocations. Compile-safe (rope_dims is a static Python int per block; static-shape graph). Sources: arxiv 2501.18795 (Rope-to-NoPE), 2502.11276 (RoPE dim-inefficiency for long-distance retrieval).

hypothesis: Byte-weighted TTT adaptation loss: weight each token's NLL by its SP byte-length (base_bytes_lut, clamped ≥1, normalized) during TTT SGD updates, aligning the adaptation objective with the BPB metric that weights by bytes not tokens; also reverts exp_029 focal loss regression back to standard CE.
notes: PR #731 [loss/low risk] uses byte-weighted TTT loss to prioritize byte-balanced adaptation. Current TTT uses flat per-token CE (misaligned with BPB). base_bytes_lut is already on GPU and indexed per target token. Longer SP tokens (more bytes) represent more semantic content and are rarer — giving them proportionally more gradient signal should improve BPB-aligned adaptation. Only the TTT adaptation phase is changed; scoring phase unchanged.

hypothesis: Per-layer TTT LR scaling: blocks 0–10 get LR scaled from 0.25× to 1.75× of ttt_lr (linear by depth), non-block params (embeddings, norms) keep 1.0×, cosine decay applied proportionally per group.
notes: Upper transformer blocks encode more domain/content-specific features and should adapt faster during score-first TTT; lower blocks encode general syntax and should change less. This concentrates TTT gradient signal where it helps most without adding any compute cost. Rebased from exp_030 (clean, no momentum reset). Scale: 0.25 at block 0 → 1.75 at block 10 (avg ≈ 1.0, same total update magnitude).

hypothesis: Fixed train_seq_len=1024 (eval_seq_len stays 2048): ~25-35% more gradient steps via 4x cheaper attention (O(T²)); training-loop eval_val now uses base_model (eager) to avoid DDP+fullgraph shape conflict that crashed exp_032.
notes: exp_032 crashed because DDP-compiled model (shape 96x1024 from warmup) rejected 32x2048 eval_val call under fullgraph=True. Fix: pass base_model (eager mode) to training-loop eval_val — any shape accepted, distributed correctness preserved via existing AllReduce inside eval_val.

hypothesis: Reduce GPTQ error-propagation block_size from 128 to 32, giving 4× more frequent Hinv correction flushes and lower accumulated quantization error, with zero artifact-size impact (per-row scales are block_size-independent).
notes: Rebased cleanly to exp_030 (no freq-weighted Hessian). Only change: gptq_quantize_weight default block_size 128→32. Total GPTQ FLOPs for global updates are O(cols^2) regardless of block_size, so eval budget impact is minimal. The seed-stack doc cited block_size=32 but the code had drifted to 128 — this corrects the drift.

hypothesis: Lower peak Muon momentum from 0.99 to 0.95 (canonical modded-nanogpt value): velocity timescale shortens from ~e^(-step/100) to e^(-step/20), giving late-warmdown steps more on-policy recent-gradient signal instead of stale accumulated velocity over the long 72% cosine warmdown.
notes: Single-knob meta sweep on a previously-untouched axis. exp_035 modified warmup *length* (0.33→0.20, discard +0.000895) but the peak value itself has never been changed. Keller Jordan canonical Muon recommends 0.85→0.95; we use 0.92→0.99. This edit lowers only the peak, keeping warmup_start=0.92 and warmup_fraction=0.33. Risk: changes core optimizer behavior across full training; could push pre-quant weights to a slightly different basin that's marginally worse for GPTQ+TTT. EV: -0.001 to -0.003 if canonical value transfers to our 5-min regime.

hypothesis: Set min_lr=0.10 so the linear warmdown LR floor is 10% of peak (not 0), preventing completely frozen-gradient final steps and letting the EMA capture meaningful training signal through end of 600s budget.
notes: Linear warmdown (exp_030 baseline) decays from LR=1.0 at frac=0.28 to LR=0.0 at frac=1.0. With min_lr=0.10, the floor hits at frac≈0.928 and the last ~43s of training run at 10% LR. Motivated by WSD literature (typical min_lr_ratio=0.1) and by exp_005 showing TTT LR floor at 0.5*ttt_lr helped (+0.002 BPB). Only the default for MIN_LR env var changed (0.0→0.1); env var still overrides.

hypothesis: Add cosine LR floor at 0.5*ttt_lr so the second half of TTT chunks (ci > num_chunks/2) adapt at 0.0025 instead of near-zero, preventing late-chunk adaptation collapse at zero eval time cost.
notes: exp_005 validated the 0.5*ttt_lr floor on the old baseline (+0.002 BPB). exp_014 (current best) has the cosine without floor. This combines the WD split from exp_014 with the LR floor from exp_005 — a zero-cost change (same gradient steps, only LR value changes). Late chunks (ci > N/2) would get LR=0.0025 instead of decaying toward 0. exp_017 showed fully flat LR (0.005 throughout) is slightly worse than cosine (1.079042 vs 1.078777), suggesting 0.5*ttt_lr as a floor is the right middle ground.

hypothesis: Per-block Muon WD split for recurrence layers (blocks 3-5): raise attn WD 0.095→0.120 and MLP WD 0.115→0.145 for the loop blocks that accumulate 3× gradient per optimizer step, while non-loop blocks keep existing WD values.
notes: Blocks 3-5 are the recurrence blocks (loop_start=3, loop_end=5, num_loops=2), called 3× per forward pass. Their gradient accumulates 3× before the Muon update, giving them proportionally higher effective training pressure. Higher WD compensates. New hyperparams muon_wd_loop=0.120 (+26% vs 0.095) and muon_wd_mlp_loop=0.145 (+26% vs 0.115). Implementation splits Muon param groups into 4: base-attn, base-mlp, loop-attn, loop-mlp. Works correctly when num_loops==0 (loop groups are empty and not added).

hypothesis: Add per-token learnable logit_bias [vocab_size=8192, init=0] to output logits before softcap, trained at tied_embed_lr with zero weight_decay — decouples token-frequency prior from the tied embedding direction so the model doesn't have to encode unigram statistics in embedding magnitudes.
notes: logit_bias is passthrough float16 in GPTQ artifact (numel=8192 <= 65536 threshold) adding ~8-12 KB compressed. Forward pass cost is one [B,T,V] + [V] broadcast add — negligible eval overhead. TTT adapts logit_bias per-chunk via the all-params SGD loop, which may help capture local frequency shifts. The bias orthogonalizes the tied embedding's two roles: input (direction matters, magnitude cancelled by RMSNorm) vs output (magnitude currently encodes frequency prior, interfering with gradient updates).

hypothesis: Add z-loss regularization (coef=1e-4) to training CE: penalizes (log Z)^2 where Z=logsumexp(logits), complementing the existing logit softcap (which bounds individual logit range ≤30) by also constraining the partition function; encourages better-calibrated logit distributions and should reduce post-GPTQ int6 quantization error.
notes: Previous z-loss attempt (exp_019, coef=1e-4) hit eval_budget_overrun on the old baseline with ttt_epochs=3. exp_030 inherits ttt_epochs=2 from exp_014, so eval budget is now clear. Z-loss is training-only (zero eval overhead). Logit softcap=30 is already present in baseline — z-loss adds a complementary constraint on the partition function magnitude rather than individual logit range.

hypothesis: Prepend a single learnable [model_dim] sink token at relative position 0 of every forward pass (dropped before the LM head so input/output shape contract is preserved); init=0 makes step-0 a no-op, then the sink learns to absorb the attention-sink probability mass that otherwise concentrates on the first real token, providing a consistent attention anchor across train and sliding-window eval windows.
notes: StreamingLLM-style sink at the embedding level (pre-block), zero-init so initial outputs match the no-sink baseline. Implementation: sink prepended after embed RMSNorm + embed_proj, x0 also includes sink, blocks operate on T+1 length, sink dropped before final_norm so logits/Hessian/loss see only T positions. Sink registered to the scalar AdamW group (named_parameters scan walks blocks only, would miss top-level params). RoPE auto-extends the cos/sin cache to T+1=2049 once; the YaRN-scale activation at 2049 vs rope_train_seq_len=2048 is a 0.05% shift in rope_base — below noise floor. Numel=512 → fp16 passthrough quant, ≈1 KB after brotli, fits comfortably in headroom (~7 KB above prior best). Risks: (a) sink + skip-pop interaction at decoder layers may inject sink into late-layer features unexpectedly — mitigated by zero init letting the model learn whether to use it; (b) if the model never finds a use for the sink it falls back to baseline behaviour (~zero impact). Targeting -0.0015 (median of literature gains for sink tokens on small LMs trained from scratch).

hypothesis: Revert exp_041 per-param-group LR to single SGD group (fixes eval overrun), add cosine LR floor at 0.5 so late-document chunks receive at least 50% of peak TTT LR instead of decaying toward zero.
notes: exp_041 overran because ~170 per-parameter SGD groups added Python overhead per optimizer.step(). Reverting to single group (like exp_030) restores budget headroom. The cosine LR floor (max(0.5, cosine_frac)) is inspired by exp_005 (keep, -0.002 from baseline) but was never applied to the exp_030 lineage — exp_031 tried floor + momentum-reset together (discard) so floor alone is untested at this quality level.

hypothesis: Warmdown-phase gradient accumulation: during the 72% LR-decay window, run 2 micro-batches (each 96 seqs at shape 96×1024) per optimizer step instead of 1, doubling the effective batch to 1536 global seqs while keeping tensor shape fixed (no torch.compile recompile). Lower gradient variance (√2) during the precision-convergence phase.
notes: Critical Batch Size theory (arxiv 2505.23971) shows optimal batch grows through training. At warmdown_frac=0.72 (72% of 600s = 432s in decay), the model spends most of training converging. 2x grad_accum halves the step count but each step is from 2x more data, targeting the CBS growth curve. No tensor shape changes, no eval impact. h.grad_accum_steps stays=1 for next_batch sizing; local_accum=2 used for the loop. Pre-warmdown phase unchanged.

hypothesis: Raise min_lr from 0.10 to 0.15: extends the LR floor phase from ~43s to ~64s of training, keeping Muon updates at 15% of peak in the final warmdown stretch.
notes: exp_045 showed min_lr=0.10 gave -0.001160 BPB improvement. Trying 0.15 to check if higher floor extends the gain. With warmdown_frac=0.72, floor hits at frac≈0.892 (last ~64s at 15% LR). Risk: diminishing returns or slight regression if the kink in the schedule is too pronounced.

hypothesis: Raise warmdown LR floor min_lr=0.10→0.15: exp_045 just established that a non-zero floor compounds well in our undertrained 600s regime (Δ=-0.001160 going from 0.0→0.10), but the curve was tested only at one point — testing 0.15 brackets which side of the peak we're on, with priors that Cerebras D2Z (arxiv 2502.15938) claims zero floor is generally optimal for under-trained models so the curve must peak then decline.
notes: Single-knob continuation of opt-domain exp_045's discovery. exp_045 changed default min_lr 0.0→0.1; this trial changes it 0.1→0.15. Pure 1-line edit on line 143. The mechanism (preserve LR signal late in warmdown so EMA/LAWA captures useful divergent snapshots) suggests the curve might still be rising past 0.10. Either outcome is high-information: still rising → push to 0.20 next; past peak → optimum lies in [0.05, 0.15].

hypothesis: Replace GPTQ row-scale std with Hessian-diagonal-weighted RMS: h_weights = H.diag()/sum normalizes input-feature importance, row_wrms = sqrt((W²) @ h_weights) gives activation-weighted effective magnitude — so rows with large weights on high-activation inputs get a scale that avoids clipping them while rows with large weights on low-activation inputs get finer quantization (AWQ principle applied to GPTQ row-scale).
notes: Replaces row_std = W_orig.std(dim=1) with row_wrms via Hessian-weighted RMS. H.diag() (post-damping, pre-permutation) gives E[a²] per input channel. Weight importance h_weights normalizes these activations; row_wrms captures which output neurons are tuned to high-activation inputs vs low-activation inputs. Block_size=128 restored from exp_030 baseline (exp_043 block_size=32 was discarded). Novel technique: AWQ-style scale but computed at GPTQ row quantization stage rather than a separate pre-processing step.

hypothesis: Add label smoothing ε=0.1 to training cross-entropy loss (F.cross_entropy label_smoothing=0.1): prevents overconfident predictions, encourages distributed representations, and nudges weights toward lower-magnitude solutions that quantize more accurately under int6 GPTQ.
notes: label_smoothing stored as model attr (self.label_smoothing); forward() is only ever called in train mode (eval path uses forward_logits directly), so this is de facto training-only. Applies also to TTT adaptation which calls base_model(x,y) in train mode -- mild regularization of TTT gradient is neutral-to-positive. Value 0.1 is the standard BERT/GPT-3 default.

hypothesis: Position-weighted training CE: tokens at later positions in each 2048-token sequence get more gradient weight (linspace 0.75→1.25, mean=1.0), concentrating training signal on context-rich predictions that align with the sliding-window eval scoring pattern.
notes: exp_022 tried a similar idea but got eval_budget_overrun on the old baseline (ttt_epochs=3). Current baseline (exp_045) has ttt_epochs=2, providing ~20s more eval headroom. The 0.75–1.25 ramp is gentle (1.67× ratio) — keeps early-position predictions meaningful while concentrating gradient on later tokens where long-context modeling matters most for BPB.

hypothesis: Add learnable affine scale (emb_norm_weight, [model_dim=512], init=1) applied after embedding RMSNorm — making it a standard affine RMSNorm at the input layer so the model can amplify/suppress per-dimension embedding signal.
notes: The current embedding pipeline uses F.rms_norm without a learnable weight (non-affine). This adds a [512] float32 vector (passthrough float16 in artifact, ~200-400 bytes compressed) and routes it through optimizer_scalar. Init=1 is identity at step-0, unlike exp_038 which used init=0 (zeroing embeddings). The scale is applied before x0 assignment so residual injection paths also see the scaled embedding. Standard affine RMSNorm consistently outperforms non-affine in literature. Eval-time overhead is one elementwise [B,T,512] multiply (negligible). emb_norm_weight has ndim=1 so restore_fp32_params will keep it in float32 during training.

hypothesis: Apply eval_temperature=0.97 sharpening to logits at scoring time in both eval_val_sliding and eval_val_ttt: GPTQ int6 quantization adds weight noise that flattens the logit distribution (underconfidence), and T=0.97 partially corrects this calibration drift at zero extra compute cost.
notes: Temperature T=0.97 divides logits by 0.97 before cross_entropy, sharpening the predicted distribution by ~3%. Rationale: quantization noise increases logit variance, flattening p(y|x) and increasing NLL above the model's true calibration. T<1 partially undoes this. Zero compute overhead — only affects final log-softmax at scoring, not TTT backward passes. If direction is wrong (model is overconfident), next step would be T=1.03.

hypothesis: Add per-head learnable XSA strength scalar (init=1.0, [num_heads]=[8] per layer) so each head can independently attenuate/amplify the exclusive-self-attention self-projection subtraction; analogous to the q_gain bifurcation (exp_030 keep) but on the XSA mechanism.
notes: Currently XSA fires uniformly at strength 1.0 on all 8 heads × 11 layers, which forces the same self-component-removal everywhere. xsa_scale gives the model freedom to ride from 0 (vanilla attention) to >1 (over-correction) per-head. Init=1.0 makes step-0 numerically identical to baseline so this can only diverge from baseline through learned signal. Total cost: 88 extra fp32 params (auto-classified as scalar via ndim<2; passthrough fp16 in artifact, ~50-80 B packed). Eval cost: one extra elementwise multiply per attention forward — orders of magnitude cheaper than exp_050's sink token (which overran). Mechanism: if any head consistently wants xsa_scale<1 the gradient will pull it down, surfacing the implicit bias of the current uniform XSA.

hypothesis: Frozen-encoder TTT: run encoder blocks {0,1,2} under torch.no_grad() then detach at enc/dec boundary so backward only propagates through decoder blocks (9 of 17 invocations), saving ~31% of per-step backward compute and preventing encoder overfitting to chunk noise.
notes: Encoder-only blocks {0,1,2} are those absent from decoder_indices=[5,3,4,5,6,7,8,9,10]. Split forward: tok_emb+blocks[0..7] run no_grad, output detached, then blocks[5,3,4,5,6,7,8,9,10] run with grad. tok_emb.weight still gets output-side gradient (logit projection). ttt_epochs=2 unchanged for budget safety.

hypothesis: Split GPTQ clip sigmas: MLP layers use mlp_clip_sigmas=15.0 (wider, preserves gate-row outliers) while attention layers keep matrix_clip_sigmas=12.85; embed unchanged at 20.0.
notes: PR #1769 validated direction: widening MLP GPTQ sigma (their 10→12) preserves high-magnitude outlier MLP weights lost to int6 clipping. Our baseline is 12.85 uniform; 15.0 for MLP is a ~17% wider clip range. Attention matrices (qkv, proj) stay at 12.85 since they have more uniform distributions. Wider MLP sigma → larger step size → Q values cluster toward 0 → compression should stay similar or improve. Two MLP matrices per block × 11 blocks = 22 matrices affected.

hypothesis: Byte-length-weighted training loss: weight each token's NLL by base_bytes_lut (clamped to ≥1, normalized to mean=1), aligning training gradient allocation with the BPB metric which weights by byte span.
notes: BPB weights each token by its byte count; training uses uniform mean CE. This mismatch means rare/long tokens (more bytes → higher BPB contribution) receive proportionally less gradient signal than they contribute to the metric. Fix: use F.cross_entropy(weight=byte_lut/byte_lut.mean()) so long tokens get proportionally more gradient. Buffer is non-persistent so at eval/TTT time (new model instance) it's all-ones = original uniform CE. GPTQ calibration and eval scoring both use forward_logits() so unaffected.

hypothesis: Raise muon_momentum_warmup_fraction 0.33→0.45: extends momentum ramp from 198s to 270s, keeping Muon momentum closer to start (0.92) longer so on-policy gradient signal dominates during early warmdown (warmdown starts at 168s, so 102s of overlap where LR is decaying AND momentum is still ramping — the fine-tuning sweet spot).
notes: Direction inferred from exp_035 (shortened to 0.20→discard +0.000895) and exp_044 (lowered peak momentum 0.99→0.95→discard +0.000754). Both "less momentum" directions hurt; exp_035's failure on the same axis (warmup-fraction) implies LONGER is the right direction. Compounds with exp_054's min_lr=0.15: LR floor extends late-training learning while a longer momentum ramp keeps the optimizer responsive to instantaneous gradients during the critical 22% overlap window where warmdown is active and momentum is still ramping. Pure 1-line constant change.

hypothesis: Replace fixed Newton-Schulz coefficients (3.4445, -4.775, 2.0315) with Polar Express per-iteration minimax-optimized coefficients (ICLR 2026), which converge to the polar factor ~2x faster in the same 5 NS steps, giving Muon higher-quality gradient orthogonalization throughout training.
notes: Polar Express coefficients from PR #1787 / arxiv 2505.16932. Per-iteration a,b,c tuples: step 0 uses (8.157,-22.483,15.879) for rapid initial normalization, then 4 refinement tuples converging to the polar factor. Drop-in replacement for the @torch.compile zeropower_via_newtonschulz5 function – no architecture change. muon_backend_steps=5 unchanged so the compile graph is still fully static.

hypothesis: Value-residual learning (ResFormer): per-kv-head lambda mixes each layer V toward layer-0 V. Init=0 so step-0 identical to baseline.
notes: torch.distributed.elastic.multiprocessing.errors.ChildFailedError: ============================================================ (train_gpt.py:1449 in step_fn)

hypothesis: Revert label_smoothing (exp_056 regression) + split scalar AdamW into WD=0 for pure norm/bias params and adam_wd=0.02 for control tensors (q_gain, skip_weight, attn_scale), following standard practice of excluding LayerNorm/bias from weight decay to prevent artificial pull toward zero.
notes: Two combined changes: (1) removes label_smoothing=0.1 from cross_entropy which was causing +4.5% BPB in exp_056; (2) novel scalar WD split: scalar_no_wd gets LayerNorm.weight/bias (1D params not matching control patterns) with weight_decay=0.0, scalar_wd gets control tensors (q_gain [H,2], attn_scale, mlp_scale, skip_gates etc.) with weight_decay=adam_wd=0.02. Mechanism: LayerNorm scale and bias should not be L2-regularized toward zero — this interferes with the model's ability to calibrate feature magnitudes. Control tensors (attention gates, skip weights) benefit from mild WD to prevent runaway scaling. The paper 'Drop Dropout on Single-Epoch Language Model Pretraining' (arxiv 2505.24788) confirms dropout hurts in single-epoch pretraining, consistent with avoiding any form of dropout augmentation in our 600s budget.

hypothesis: Add learnable bigram shortcut: bigram_alpha (scalar, zero-init) injects the previous-token normalized embedding into the current position, giving the first layers an explicit causal bigram signal and freeing MLP capacity from bigram statistics per Bigram Subnetworks NeurIPS 2025.
notes: Uses torch.cat([zeros, x[:,:-1]], dim=1) shifted embedding; multiplied by bigram_alpha registered in scalar optimizer. Zero-init means transparent at step 0. Only adds 1 float32 scalar (2 bytes in artifact after float16 passthrough). No new quantized matrix. Should not affect size or eval budget.

hypothesis: MiLe Loss (ICLR 2025): replace flat CE with entropy-weighted CE — per-token loss scaled by (1+H(p))/mean(1+H(p)) where H(p)=−∑p·logp is the model's predicted-distribution entropy (stop-grad), up-weighting positions with genuine multi-token ambiguity while down-weighting already-confident predictions.
notes: MiLe Loss paper (ICLR 2025, arxiv 2310.19531) shows consistent downstream improvements at 468M/1.2B/6.7B. Key distinction from focal loss: focal uses (1-p_correct)^gamma (wrong-prediction weight), MiLe uses (1+H(p))^gamma (distribution-uncertainty weight). For undertrained 600s model with high average entropy, MiLe focuses gradient on genuinely ambiguous positions. Implementation: log_softmax->exp->sum inside torch.no_grad() to compute stop-grad entropy; normalize batch-wise so effective loss scale ≈ CE. Rebased from exp_054 (min_lr=0.15) not exp_057 (position-weighted).

hypothesis: Skip non-metric eval_val_sliding (saves ~100s) by defaulting sliding_window_enabled=0, decouple TTT from that flag, and restore ttt_epochs=3 (original baseline value, forced to 2 in exp_014 to clear budget); the freed eval budget more than covers the extra TTT adaptation epoch cost (~4% overhead).
notes: eval_val_sliding contributes zero to final val_bpb (primary metric is quantized_ttt). Decoupling it from the TTT condition allows TTT to run even when sliding_window_enabled=0. Extra TTT epoch: ~1 backward per chunk vs ~16 forward passes for scoring → ~4% more TTT wall. Saves ~100s from skipping sliding eval, costs ~17s for extra epoch, net -83s. Eval should land ~492-512s total.

hypothesis: Byte-length-weighted training loss (v2): manual reduction="none" + gather + weighted sum, fixing fullgraph=True incompatibility with F.cross_entropy(weight=...) from exp_063.
notes: exp_063 used F.cross_entropy(weight=byte_lut) which caused torch.compile(fullgraph=True) failure (BPB 2.307, eval overrun). Fix: compute loss manually as nll=F.cross_entropy(reduction='none'), w=byte_weight_lut[targets].float(), return (nll*w).sum()/w.sum(). This is fullgraph-compatible: reduction='none' is standard, tensor-indexing is a gather op, elementwise mult and sum are trivial. Non-persistent buffer stays all-ones at eval/TTT time so those paths are unaffected. Same byte-weighting hypothesis: align training gradient with BPB metric.

hypothesis: Reduce GPTQ Hessian damping from 1% to 0.5% of H.diag().mean(): lower regularization bias in the Cholesky H-inverse yields more accurate per-column error compensation, at zero compute overhead.
notes: Isolates the damping-reduction component of exp_015 (which bundled damp 0.01→0.005 with block_size 128→64 and got eval_budget_overrun from the block_size change). Act-order is already implemented (columns sorted by descending H diagonal), so lower damping is numerically safe — highest-variance columns are processed first where the inverse is most stable. PR #1318 validated that damp005 with freq-weighted GPTQ improves BPB; this tests the damping component alone on our current best baseline.

hypothesis: Replace linear LR warmdown with cosine warmdown (min_lr=0.15 floor preserved): with linear decay LR hits the floor at frac≈0.892 (~64s before end), wasting learning signal; cosine decay stays above the floor until frac=1.0 exactly, keeping LR higher throughout warmdown for more gradient signal while converging to the same min_lr endpoint.
notes: lr_mul now: t=(frac-(1-warmdown_frac))/warmdown_frac; return min_lr + (1-min_lr)*0.5*(1+cos(pi*t)). With warmdown_frac=0.72 and min_lr=0.15, cosine gives ~15% higher LR at mid-warmdown vs linear. Linear hit floor 64s early; cosine avoids this entirely.

hypothesis: Raise muon_momentum_warmup_fraction 0.45→0.55: extend Muon momentum ramp 270s→330s, increasing warmdown-overlap-with-ramping-momentum from 102s→162s — pushes the same axis exp_064 just kept (0.20 discard, 0.45 keep, longer-ramp-is-better trend).
notes: exp_044 (0.99→0.95 peak momentum) discarded +0.000754 — the system prefers HIGH peak momentum, so the only remaining lever for "more on-policy late training" is a longer ramp, not a lower peak. exp_064 (0.33→0.45) just kept -0.000038. At fraction=0.55, momentum at warmdown-start (frac=0.28=168s) is 0.92+(168/330)*0.07=0.956 vs current 0.964 — modestly lower, slightly more recent-gradient weighting during the productive early-warmdown window. Risk: diminishing returns mean this could land just below noise floor or slight regression; if so, this axis is exhausted.

hypothesis: Equalize TTT gradient magnitudes for recurrence blocks 3-5: they execute (num_loops+1)=3× per forward pass so their .grad accumulates 3× the magnitude of once-executed blocks; rescaling by 1/3 after backward() gives uniform effective TTT learning rate across all 11 blocks.
notes: Mechanistic fix: with num_loops=2, blocks 3-5 run 3 times per forward pass. Accumulated .grad is 3x larger than single-pass blocks. Without correction, effective TTT LR for blocks 3-5 is 3*ttt_lr=0.015 vs 0.005 for other blocks. Rescaling by _lsc=1/3 equalizes this. Zero compute overhead (one named_parameters() scan + mul_ per backward step). No extra optimizer state. No architecture changes.

hypothesis: Value-residual learning (ResFormer): per-kv-head lambda mixes each layer V toward layer-0 V. Init=0 so step-0 identical to baseline. Always-apply formulation so v_lambda is in autograd graph for DDP.
notes: Re-attempt of exp_066 which crashed with DDP unused-param. Fix: always apply lam*(v_first-v); on layer 0 v_first=v makes it zero so behavior is no-op but lambda is referenced. Threaded through depth recurrence.

hypothesis: Add weight_decay=0.1 to TTT SGD optimizer: regularizes chunk-to-chunk weight drift, preventing early-chunk over-adaptation from degrading later-chunk predictions.
notes: TTT SGD currently has no WD. Adding ttt_wd=0.1 gives (1 - lr*wd) = 0.9995 per step at max LR=0.005, accumulating to ~1.5% decay over ~30 total TTT steps across all chunks. This pulls each chunk's adapted weights slightly back toward the GPTQ-quantized starting point, reducing over-specialization. PRs #1767/#1765 showed TTT WD=1.0 helped for LoRA-TTT adapters; we extend this to full-model TTT with a conservative value. No effect on training (eval-time only).

hypothesis: Hierarchical sliding-window attention on early encoder layers 1, 2 (window=512 of seq_len=2048) via FA3 window_size; all other layers stay full causal. Zero new params; biases early-encoder receptive field toward local n-gram aggregation while leaving the recurrence (layers 3-5) and decoder (6-10) globally attentive. Layer 0 keeps full attention to preserve the attention-sink mechanism.
notes: FA3 supports window_size=(left,0) for causal+local; SDPA fallback builds an explicit additive mask. Static Python int swa_window per CausalSelfAttention so torch.compile resolves the conditional at trace time → no graph break. Zero param-count delta vs exp_075, so artifact size unchanged. Mistral / Gemma-style hierarchical schedule, layer-0 untouched per the "When Attention Sink Emerges" empirical guidance (ICLR 2025) that early sink behavior matters. | torch.distributed.elastic.multiprocessing.errors.ChildFailedError: ============================================================ (train_gpt.py:91 in flash_attn_3_func)

hypothesis: Add a learnable [model_dim=512] word-start structural embedding (zero-init) that is added to the post-RMSNorm input representation for all word-initial tokens (those whose SP piece starts with ▁), encoding the linguistic word-boundary prior explicitly so the transformer can focus embedding capacity on token-specific content rather than re-learning the shared word-start bias from co-occurrence patterns.
notes: Word-initial tokens (▁word) have a systematic structural role distinct from word-continuation tokens (##ing). Currently the 512-dim embedding table has separate rows for each token, requiring gradient descent to learn the shared word-start signal implicitly. A single zero-init [512] vector added only for is_word_start tokens makes this explicit and parameter-efficient (512×2=1024 bytes float16, ~800 bytes brotli). The has_leading_space_lut is a persistent=False buffer rebuilt from SP at init, so it adds 0 bytes to the artifact. The word_start_emb is added to scalar_params (AdamW, lr=scalar_lr=0.02).

hypothesis: 2-step-ahead auxiliary CE loss (α=0.15): at position t, co-train the same tied-embedding projection to predict x_{t+2} in addition to x_{t+1}, adding a richer look-ahead gradient signal with zero extra artifact parameters.
notes: Zero extra params — reuses tied embedding. Logits[:, :-1] vs target_ids[:, 1:] gives the 2-step target. Combined gradient at each position is ~93% next-token + ~7% next-next-token, equivalent to principled label smoothing toward the actual upcoming content. Differs from MiLe/focal/z-loss (which reweight existing CE) — this adds a new target. Concern: MTP literature shows small-model benefit is weak; but our shared-head formulation differs from the independent-head MTP studied there. Alpha=0.15 keeps the auxiliary signal subordinate to the main objective.

hypothesis: Frequency-weighted GPTQ Hessian calibration: pre-collect all calibration batches, count token frequencies, boost top-100 most-frequent tokens by sqrt(2) in H=X^TX, biasing GPTQ to minimize quantization error on common tokens. This technique improved bpb by -0.001812 on the old baseline (exp_002) but was never merged into the main lineage.
notes: Port of exp_002 technique onto exp_064. exp_002 was a direct child of baseline (exp_000) and got val_bpb=1.079188 vs baseline 1.081000 = -0.001812. The main lineage (exp_014→030→045→054→064) never incorporated this Hessian improvement. Rebased cleanly from exp_064 so damping stays at 0.01 (not the 0.005 from discarded exp_072).

hypothesis: Per-chunk NLL-adaptive TTT learning rate: after score-first evaluation of each chunk, scale the cosine-scheduled adaptation LR by clip(chunk_nll/running_mean_nll, 0.5, 2.0), reallocating adaptation budget toward chunks where the model is struggling most.
notes: Mechanism: compute per-chunk average NLL during the existing score-first pass (free, already in no_grad). All-reduce across GPUs. Update running mean. Scale cos_lr by nll_scale = chunk_nll/mean, clamped [0.5,2.0]. Rationale: chunks with high NLL are novel/hard content where TTT adaptation yields most gain; chunks with low NLL are already well-predicted and need less aggressive LR. The cosine schedule provides the base decay envelope. Overhead: 2 extra all_reduce per chunk ~50ms total. No change to scoring phase or training data used.

hypothesis: Lower embed_wd from 0.085 to 0.05 on tied tok_emb/lm_head: a previously untouched meta knob; the Power Lines paper (arxiv 2505.13738) shows optimal WD scales inversely with model size at fixed tokens-per-parameter, and our 600s budget is heavily undertrained, so the embedding (which doubles as output projection due to tying) is being over-regularized at 0.085.
notes: Rebased to exp_064 first (workdir was at 0.55 momentum-warmup-fraction from the discarded exp_074). Single-line edit: line 165 default 0.085→0.05. Affects only tok_emb.weight via optimizer_tok AdamW (line 842). embed_wd has never been moved from its multi_596-seed value in any meta/opt/reg trial — genuinely uncrowded axis. 0.05 chosen as a moderate decrease (41%) comparable in radius to the 0.10→0.15 min_lr keep that compounded; sits between adam_wd=0.02 and muon_wd=0.095. Embeddings are the bottleneck for rare-token BPB (every position routes through them); less WD pressure preserves more learned magnitude in the undertrained regime.

hypothesis: Replace SGD with AdamW (betas=(0.9,0.95), wd=0) in TTT inner loop: AdamW's second-moment normalization automatically equalizes per-parameter step sizes across heterogeneous gradient scales (embedding vs deep MLP), generalizing the manual recurrence-block gradient scaling from exp_075 to all layer types.
notes: Optimizer step cost is negligible vs forward/backward cost in TTT (2 epochs × ~2 seqs/GPU × 22 chunks = 88 steps total), so AdamW overhead is <1% of eval time. Second moment persists across chunks, building an increasingly accurate per-parameter variance estimate. ttt_lr=0.005 kept; AdamW step ≈ ±lr in first iteration (sign-SGD-like), which should help parameters with varying gradient magnitudes adapt more uniformly.

hypothesis: Lower ema_decay from 0.9965 to 0.99 (EMA window ~100 steps ≈ 2.5% of ~4000-step run), focusing weight averaging on the LR-floor convergence phase; LAWA paper (arxiv 2306.03241) shows ~1% window is optimal. Also restores linear warmdown (reverting exp_073 cosine).
notes: EMA decay 0.9965→0.99 gives tighter window (100 vs 286 steps) so final EMA model better represents the fully-converged floor phase. Linear warmdown revert ensures we're building on exp_064 not exp_073.

hypothesis: Replace per-shard random permutation with Weyl (golden-ratio) quasi-random permutation cycled across resets: each shard reset starts at a different offset in the golden-ratio sequence, ensuring maximally uniform positional coverage even when training terminates mid-epoch.
notes: Random permutations on each shard reset are independent draws; with ~600s wall-clock and multiple resets per shard, some positions risk systematic under-sampling. Weyl sequence rotated by golden-ratio * N on each reset guarantees that over K resets, positional coverage is within ⌈K/N⌉ visits per slot (vs Poisson variance for random). Pure data-ordering change: no loss modification, no model change, no eval-path impact — zero risk of crash or eval_budget_overrun.

hypothesis: Lower Muon WD by 0.65× for recurrence loop blocks (3-5) only: loop blocks get 3× gradient per forward pass so their effective gradient-to-WD ratio is already 3× that of non-loop blocks; reducing their WD (attn 0.095→0.062, mlp 0.115→0.075) lets them find sharper minima without WD opposing the strong accumulated gradient signal.
notes: Opposite direction from exp_047 (which tried HIGHER WD for loop blocks and regressed +0.001). The mechanistic asymmetry: loop blocks accumulate 3x gradient per backward pass through the 3-loop recurrence (loop_start=3, loop_end=5, num_loops=2). Standard WD (same for all blocks) over-regularizes these blocks relative to their gradient magnitude. muon_wd_loop_mult=0.65 gives loop attn WD=0.0618, loop MLP WD=0.0748. Non-loop blocks unchanged. Guard: only splits when num_loops > 0 (always true in baseline).

hypothesis: Hierarchical sliding-window attention on early encoder layers 1, 2 (window=512 of seq_len=2048) via FA3 window_size; layer 0 + recurrence + decoder stay full causal. Zero new params; biases early-encoder receptive field toward local n-gram aggregation. Re-submit of #078 with SDPA fallback fixed (smoke ignores window_size — zero-param edit, artifact size unaffected) plus FA3-signature probe so an older flash_attn_interface gracefully degrades to full attention.
notes: Crash post-mortem of #078: smoke-phase SDPA fallback raised at the explicit attn_mask path (additive -inf with bf16 mask + masked_fill_). Fix: smoke fallback ignores window_size and uses full causal — pack-size measurement is unaffected because SWA adds zero parameters. Plus an inspect.signature probe on _fa3_impl so a future / different flash_attn_interface install also degrades safely instead of TypeError'ing on the kwarg. Hierarchical schedule: layer 0 keeps sink, layers 1-2 local 512, layers 3-10 global. Mistral / Gemma precedent.

hypothesis: Add learnable per-dimension output scale (output_dim_scale, [model_dim=512], init=1) applied after final_norm but before the tied-embedding logit projection, learning a diagonal metric in the output space to reweight which embedding dimensions matter most for next-token prediction — unlike exp_058's input-side scale (cancelled by input RMSNorm), this scale directly modulates the logit similarity computation.
notes: Mechanistic rationale: tied embeddings have dual roles (input representation + output classifier). After final_norm, the 512-dim hidden state has unit RMS but per-dimension variance differs. output_dim_scale=[512] (init=1) reweights dimensions before F.linear(x, tok_emb.weight), learning which context dimensions most predict token identity. Equivalent to learning diag(s) between context and embedding. Init=1 → step-0 identical to baseline. Removes the failed word_start_emb from exp_079 and the SP scan overhead.

hypothesis: Raise ema_decay 0.9965→0.998: extends EMA half-life 198→346 steps to better average the long warmdown_frac=0.72 trajectory now that min_lr=0.15 produces stable, meaningfully-moving late steps.
notes: Fresh meta axis — tree.tsv shows no prior trial touched ema_decay. Image-classification literature (Morales-Brotons et al. 2024 arxiv 2411.18704) tests α∈{0.968,0.984,0.992,0.996,0.998}; we're at 0.9965, below the upper-empirical cluster at 0.996-0.998. Compounds with exp_045 (min_lr=0.10) and exp_054 (min_lr=0.15) which together established that the warmdown trajectory keeps moving meaningfully — more averaging extracts more signal from these many low-noise late steps. Risk: too much smoothing biases EMA toward middle-training weights when undertrained model is still improving. One-knob change, ~0.0015 absolute step on a saturating axis (1-α from 0.0035 to 0.002, ~43% reduction).

hypothesis: Nesterov SGD + per-chunk momentum reset for TTT: switch SGD to nesterov=True (lookahead gradient computes at θ-lr·v, more accurate in the ~6-step-per-chunk regime) and clear optimizer.state at each chunk boundary (removes stale velocity from the previous chunk's different distribution).
notes: Two complementary eval-domain changes: (1) nesterov=True gives lookahead correction per step — prevents overshooting especially with only ~6 TTT steps per chunk; (2) optimizer.state.clear() before each chunk's adaptation ensures Nesterov velocity from the prior chunk (a different topic distribution) doesn't contaminate the fresh chunk. Both target the same mechanism: cleaner, higher-quality per-chunk adaptation with the same eval time budget.

hypothesis: Combine exp_075's recurrence-block gradient equalization (rescale blocks 3-5 grad by 1/(num_loops+1)=1/3 after backward) with Nesterov momentum (nesterov=True) in TTT SGD, on top of exp_082's NLL-adaptive LR — both improvements were on separate lineage branches and have never been combined.
notes: exp_082 (eval specialist) has NLL-adaptive LR but NOT gradient equalization. exp_075 (ttt specialist) has gradient equalization but NOT NLL-adaptive LR. This submission stacks both on exp_082 and additionally enables Nesterov momentum. Nesterov weights current-chunk gradient 1.9x vs old buffer 0.81x (vs plain momentum 1.0x vs 0.9x), which is beneficial for TTT where each chunk has a partially different distribution. Expected delta approximately -0.00015 from additive combination of the two improvements.

hypothesis: Restore muon_momentum_warmup_fraction 0.33→0.45 in exp_082 baseline: the eval specialist submitted exp_082 from a stale workdir that did not inherit exp_064's validated 0.45 setting, so the current best runs Muon with only 198s of momentum warmup instead of the 270s shown to be optimal by exp_064 (Δ=-0.000038 on prior lineage).
notes: exp_064 demonstrated this exact change (0.33→0.45) gave Δ=-0.000038. exp_082 (best) inadvertently dropped this by using a non-rebased workdir. This edit adds it back on top of exp_082's NLL-adaptive TTT LR. Changes are independent: momentum ramp affects training (first 270s), TTT LR scaling affects eval-time adaptation. Combined effect should be approximately additive.

hypothesis: Per-token loss clipping at 5.0 nats: replace mean CE with mean(clamp(per_token_CE, max=5.0)), removing gradient from positions where the model gives <0.7% probability to the correct token, so the optimizer focuses on achievable improvements rather than noise-dominated impossible positions.
notes: All previous loss modifications in this lineage hurt (z-loss +0.005, focal +0.009, position-weight +0.002, entropy-weight +0.004, 2-step-ahead +0.044). This is the only genuinely novel direction: removing gradient entirely from the hardest ~10% of tokens (loss>5.0 nats) rather than reweighting them. Mechanism: gradient is zero for clamped positions, so optimizer budget concentrates on the 90% of tokens where improvement is achievable. TTT adaptation is also affected (no gradient on hard tokens during TTT), which may help stability or slightly hurt rare-token adaptation.

hypothesis: Add MLP hidden dropout (p=0.02) applied after LeakyReLU²-square activation before output projection: first true dropout mechanism in this run (exp_036 was stochastic depth), zero→nonzero qualitative change; disabled during eval/TTT via model.eval() → training=False, so only training dynamics are affected while quantization/scoring are clean.
notes: MLP hidden dim=2048; p=0.02 means 98% of neurons active per step on average. F.dropout preserves expected value via scale-by-1/(1-p). The 11 loop+non-loop blocks all get identical dropout_p. Rationale: distributed MLP activations are more robust to int6 quantization (which selectively kills small weights, analogous to dropout). muon_wd_mlp=0.115 already biases toward MLP regularization; small dropout doubles down without hurting capacity severely given ~4000 training steps.

hypothesis: Split attention vs MLP clip sigmas: attention layers (c_q/c_k/c_v/proj) use attn_clip_sigmas=10.0 instead of the uniform 12.85, giving 22% finer int6 quantization grid for attn weights that have few outliers beyond 10σ, while MLP layers stay at 12.85 where the wider range is justified by exp_062's regression when MLP was widened to 15.0.
notes: PR #1769 uses attn clip=10.0 as its default baseline before raising MLP to 12.0 — empirical evidence that 10.0 works for attention. Our attn is at 12.85 (same as MLP), which is wider than needed for near-Gaussian attn weights in a small undertrained 512-dim model. Tighter clip = finer quantization of the bulk with negligible clipping cost. The classify_param() function already distinguishes mlp vs attn, so routing is clean. Zero size impact — only changes quantization fidelity, not scale tensor format or count.

hypothesis: Headwise query-dependent sigmoid output gate (Qwen NeurIPS 2025 Best Paper, arxiv 2505.06708) on attention, restricted to the 3 recurrence-loop blocks (layers 3,4,5). Gate logits ride free on extended c_q output (no separate matmul); identity-init via gate_bias=5.0 (sigmoid≈0.993); applied element-wise per head between SDPA and o_proj. Recurrence amplifies the effect (each gated block traversed 3× per forward pass).
notes: Structurally novel: untried in this swarm and absent from the PR library. Mechanism per Qwen paper: introduces query-dependent non-linearity on the low-rank softmax output, eliminates attention sink and massive activations. Targeting only loop blocks (a) keeps artifact size delta to ~3-6 KB (3 layers × 4096 weights in extended c_q at int6+brotli), and (b) puts the gate where each block is replayed 3× per forward pass — per-pass query-dependent suppression of noisy heads is exactly where stability gains compound. Identity-init means step-0 is bit-equivalent to baseline (gate_bias=5 → sigmoid≈0.993, plus proj is zero-init). gate_bias is ndim=1 → auto-classified as scalar (Adam, scalar_lr=0.02). Risk: tight size headroom — if size_blocked, fall back to gate on layer 5 only or set LOWBIT_LAYERS for blocks.{3,4,5}.attn.c_q to int5.

hypothesis: At warmdown start (frac=0.28), reset all per-rank shard orderings with fresh random phases and permutations so the 432s LR-decay convergence phase operates on a fully re-randomized data schedule rather than the tail of the initial epoch's permutation.
notes: Mechanism: _warmdown_reshuffled flag triggers _reset_shard() for all files on each rank once at frac >= 1-warmdown_frac=0.28. Each shard gets a new random phase in [0, seq_len-1] and a new random permutation of its sequences. The ~28% of data consumed in the high-LR phase used one set of orderings; the warmdown EMA phase (which produces the final model) now uses a completely independent set. No tensor shape changes, no torch.compile interaction. PR#684 identified adaptive shard-mix as a useful curr technique; this is a simpler variant applying a single re-randomization at the most impactful moment.

hypothesis: Raise grad_clip_norm 0.3→0.5: an untouched meta knob in this entire 90-trial run; 0.3 is unusually tight (modern recipes use 1.0 default), and since Muon row_normalize already bounds matrix updates, this clip primarily constrains AdamW scalar/embed updates including the bifurcated qk_gain [H,2] (exp_030's biggest recent gain) — loosening lets scalar params take larger steps during peak-LR phase when grads are most informative.
notes: Mid-radius lift (1.67x). Web search confirmed 1.0 is standard default; 0.3 is conservative legacy. Going to 0.5 (not 1.0) limits stability risk. Mechanism affects peak-LR phase most; warmdown grads are small enough that clip is rarely binding there. Watch for: (a) train_budget_overrun if optimizer step time changes (unlikely), (b) train instability NaN. Untouched knob → low collision risk with parallel agents.

hypothesis: Discriminative per-layer TTT learning rates: tok_emb×0.5, blocks 0-2×0.3, blocks 3-7×0.7, blocks 8-10+final_norm×1.0; combined with existing NLL-adaptive cosine scaling so upper layers (most document-specific) adapt more aggressively each chunk.
notes: Discriminative fine-tuning (ULMFiT-style) applied to TTT: upper transformer layers handle higher-level semantic composition that is more document-specific, lower layers handle universal syntactic patterns. Per-layer LR multipliers concentrate the TTT adaptation budget where it is most useful. Multipliers: tok_emb=0.5 (tied embedding/logit head, conservative to avoid vocab drift), blocks 0-2=0.3 (early universal layers), blocks 3-7=0.7 (mid layers including recurrence loop), blocks 8-10+final_norm=1.0 (upper semantic layers). Each chunk still gets the existing NLL-adaptive cosine LR scale from exp_082, so the decomposition is: effective_lr = ttt_lr × cosine_factor × nll_scale × layer_mult. Zero extra compute cost — just param grouping in the SGD optimizer.

hypothesis: Replace standard CE with focal CE (γ=2) in TTT adaptation step: down-weight tokens the model predicts confidently (focal_w=(1-p_t)^2 ≈ 0) and concentrate gradient signal on hard tokens that inflate BPB most, making each TTT step more efficient per chunk.
notes: Focal loss γ=2 is the standard choice from RetinaNet. In TTT context: easy tokens (p_t≈1) get near-zero gradient weight, hard tokens (p_t≈0) get full weight. This concentrates the limited per-chunk adaptation budget on the tokens the model struggles with. Score phase unchanged (still standard CE). Backward still through full model (same topology as exp_092). Only the loss weighting changes.

hypothesis: Pre-norm bigram injection: add previous token's raw embedding (scaled by learnable scalar bigram_alpha, zero-init) to the current token embedding BEFORE RMSNorm, so the normalized direction encodes (prev, cur) bigram pair geometry — differs from exp_068's post-norm normalized-prev injection by baking the bigram signal into the normalized direction itself.
notes: Rebase to exp_082. Added bigram_alpha (scalar, zeros init) to GPT model, applied as x = x + alpha*prev_x before F.rms_norm. prev_x = F.pad(x[:,:-1,:], (0,0,1,0)) is causally correct. bigram_alpha added to scalar_params in Optimizers so it is trained at scalar_lr=0.02 with adam_wd=0.02. This is the pre-norm variant of bigram mixing (exp_068 tried post-norm normalized-prev, was discarded). Zero parameter size overhead (1 scalar = 2 bytes float16). Zero eval time overhead.

hypothesis: Extend warmdown_frac from 0.72→0.76: with min_lr=0.15 floor both schedules reach the floor at ~533s, but the shallower linear slope gives the EMA more time accumulating near-converged weights, better matching the hard-task WSD regime where longer decay fractions are optimal.
notes: warmdown_frac 0.72→0.76 shifts peak-phase from 168s to 144s (-24s), adds 24s more to the smooth linear decay. Floor is still hit at ~532s (3s earlier than current 535s), floor duration 68s vs 65s. The decay slope shallows from 0.85/432=0.00197/s to 0.85/456=0.00186/s, giving slightly lower LR throughout decay which should reduce gradient noise during EMA accumulation. Nobody in the opt lineage has explicitly touched warmdown_frac.

hypothesis: Revert exp_094's catastrophic per_token.clamp(max=5.0) and replace with F.cross_entropy label_smoothing=0.01: at vocab=8192 the clamp silenced all gradients at init (ln(8192)≈9>>5), and ε=0.01 micro-smoothing may marginally improve post-GPTQ calibration without measurable gradient distortion.
notes: Previous label smoothing (exp_056, regression) was at unknown larger coefficient. ε=0.01 with V=8192 shifts non-target probability by only 1.2e-6 per token, making the gradient signal nearly identical to plain CE while forcing slightly softer output distributions that may be more robust to int6 quantization noise.

hypothesis: Split Muon WD within attention block by functional role: Q/K routing matrices get muon_wd_qk=0.075 (lower, needs more optimization freedom), V/O content matrices get muon_wd_vo=0.110 (higher, functions like MLP projection); analogous to the successful attn/MLP WD split (exp_013) but applied within attn itself.
notes: Q/K determine attention routing patterns (interacts with RoPE, more structured); V/O extract and project content (functionally closer to MLP). Average attn WD=(0.075+0.110)/2=0.0925≈unchanged from 0.095. 3 Muon param groups instead of 2. WD tweaks for loop-blocks (exp_047/087) failed due to positional not functional grouping; this functional split is more analogous to the working attn/MLP split.

hypothesis: Peri-LN (ICLR 2025, arxiv 2502.02732): add a parameter-free RMSNorm to each sublayer's OUTPUT before the residual add — sandwich-style y = x + scale * RMSNorm(Module(RMSNorm(x))) — bounding per-sublayer contribution magnitude in both sequential (layers 0-6) and parallel-residual (layers 7-10) modes.
notes: Targets the depth-amplification pain point: blocks 3-5 are looped (num_loops+1)=3× per forward pass, so unbounded sublayer outputs compound across loop iterations. Peri-LN caps each invocation's residual contribution. Zero params added (F.rms_norm is parameter-free); attn_scale/mlp_scale [dim] already serve as post-norm gamma. Step-0 preserved (proj zero-init → attn_out=0 → RMSNorm(0)=0). bf16 default eps (~7.8e-3) damps the small-input transition smoothly. Untried in our swarm history (no prior post-sublayer norm trial in tree.tsv). Should not trigger eval_budget_overrun like exp_097/exp_088: ~34 RMSNorm ops are negligible vs SDPA/MLP cost. Stability hedge: if attn_scale=1 init proves too aggressive given RMSNorm's RMS≈1 cap, follow-up could halve attn_scale/mlp_scale init.

hypothesis: Raise scalar_lr 0.02→0.03 (50% bump) on the AdamW group containing the bifurcated q_gain[H,2] (exp_030's biggest +Δ), attn_scale/mlp_scale, resid_mix, and skip_gates — an entirely untouched meta knob currently identical to matrix_lr=0.022, while modern muP/normalized-transformer recipes give scalar gains materially higher LR than matrices because they are few-parameter and need fast convergence in our undertrained 600s regime.
notes: Untouched axis verified via tree.tsv grep across all 99 trials. Risk: AdamW 2nd-moment normalization bounds step size; adam_wd=0.02 decoupled WD scales with LR but effective WD step (~0.0009) stays in healthy regime. If +0.0003 to +0.001 we'll know scalar group needs slower (not faster) LR; either result is informative for the unexplored per-group LR axis.

hypothesis: Split GPTQ clip sigmas: attention layers (c_q/c_k/c_v/proj) use attn_clip_sigmas=10.0 vs uniform 12.85, giving a finer int6 quantization grid for attn weights (few outliers beyond 10σ) while MLP layers keep 12.85 to preserve their wider-tail outliers.
notes: Exp_096 applied this same change to exp_075 and got bpb=1.076026 (Δ≈-0.000557 vs its parent) but was size_blocked. Applied here to exp_092 whose differently-trained weights may compress differently. Code overhead is minimal (25 bytes extra after LZMA).

hypothesis: Dynamic small→large batch schedule: use half-size batch (393k tokens) for the pre-warmdown phase (0–28% of training), then switch to full batch (786k tokens) at warmdown start; pre-compile both shapes during warmup to avoid mid-training recompile, yielding ~25% more optimizer steps early when critical batch size is small.
notes: Motivated by CBS paper (arxiv 2505.23971): critical batch size starts near 0 and grows during training, so small batches are fully efficient early on. Half batch (393k) at GPU speeds would give ~2100 steps in first 168s instead of ~1333, then 2880 steps in remaining 432s at full batch (warmdown phase). Total ~5000 steps vs ~4000 baseline (+25%). Both shapes pre-compiled in warmup (free cost). batch_phase1_frac=0.28 matches warmdown start (1-0.72=0.28).

hypothesis: Switch warmdown LR shape from linear to sqrt: replace (1-frac)/warmdown_frac with sqrt((1-frac)/warmdown_frac), keeping LR higher through most of warmdown (e.g. at 50% through: 0.707 vs 0.5) and deferring the min_lr floor hit from ~535s to ~590s, giving more training time at productive learning rates.
notes: sqrt((1-frac)/warmdown_frac) is the "1-sqrt" decay shape from WSD literature that outperforms linear in hard-task pretraining regimes (arxiv 2602.06797, 2511.18903). With warmdown_frac=0.72, min_lr=0.15: linear hits floor at 535s (leaving 65s frozen at 0.15), sqrt hits floor at 590s (only 10s frozen). More productive updates through the full budget.

hypothesis: Halve TTT chunk size from 32K to 16K tokens: 2× more adaptation events with the same total budget, making nll_scale more responsive to FineWeb's domain-shift structure; the cosine LR schedule normalizes by num_chunks so per-token LR envelope is identical.
